headline: Wealth Gap: Top 10% Own 58.4% While Bottom 50% Own 7.6%
context: The Credit Suisse Global Wealth Report 2025 shows Malaysia's top 10% own 58.4% of national wealth while the bottom 50% own 7.6%, with the gap widening across all ethnic groups.

cards.big: Bottom 50% of Malaysians own 7.6% of wealth. Top 10% own 58.4%.
cards.sub: The wealth gap cuts across all races. It is a class divide dressed in ethnic clothing.

cards.big: Intra-Bumiputera wealth inequality is higher than inter-ethnic inequality.
cards.sub: The Gini coefficient within Bumiputera (0.39) exceeds the coefficient between ethnic groups (0.34).

cards.big: Race-based policies address the inter-ethnic gap (0.34) but ignore the intra-ethnic gap (0.39).
cards.sub: The larger problem is structurally invisible to the policy framework.

cards.big: B40 households across all races share identical barriers: education, capital, healthcare.
cards.sub: Race determines access to assistance. Class determines the need. These are different distributions.

cards.big: The biggest inequality in Malaysia is within communities, not between them. Race-based policy is solving the smaller problem.
Malaysia's inequality is primarily class-based. A needs-based framework would help more B40 Bumiputera than the current race-based system — while also helping B40 non-Bumiputera. Both facts matter.
